articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the BOCW Act and Cess Act, enacted in 1996, remained largely unimplemented in many states until Welfare Boards were constituted under Section 18. The Court held that the lack of machinery (Welfare Boards) led to the dormancy of these laws. The ruling highlights the need for state-level enforcement of labour welfare provisions for construction workers.

articles.title: Transfer of Member Interest in No Share Capital Company
articles.summary: The Companies Act, 2013 mandates that transfers of member interest in companies without share capital must be executed through a proper instrument signed by both the transferor and transferee and delivered to the company. This requirement ensures legal compliance and transparency in such transactions. The rule applies to all no-share capital companies operating in India.
articles.raw_summary: Section 56 of Companies Act, 2013 requires execution of a proper instrument of transfer for transfer of interest of a member in a company not having share capital. articles.title: Delhi High Court Issues Notice On PIL Challenging Consumer Protection Act Provisions Relating To  'Substantial Question Of Law'
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court , comprising Chief Justice Devendra Kumar Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia , has issued notice to the Union of India in a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) challenging the constitutional validity of provisions of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019. The plea challenges provisions which vest the National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (NCDRC) with the power to adjudicate “substantial questions of law” through benches that may include technical and non-judicial members.

articles.title: Supreme Court Quarterly Digest 2026 - Environmental Law
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, in the case Rhythm County v. Satish Sanjay, ruled that the Central Pollution Control Board's (CPCB) methodology for environmental compensation is a facilitative tool and not a rigid code. The court held that while the methodology is primarily designed for industrial sectors, its adoption by the National Green Tribunal (NGT) for residential projects is legally permissible if the quantification is not arbitrary or disproportionate.

A report.

articles.title: Accrued Income Cannot Be Deferred Just Because Receipt Is Delayed – ITAT Upholds Taxability on Mercantile Basis
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that interest income must be taxed on an accrual basis under the mercantile accounting system, regardless of delayed receipt. The assessee failed to disclose the full accrued amount in the relevant year, leading to reassessment and tax addition. The ruling reinforces the principle that taxability is determined by accrual, not actual receipt, for businesses following mercantile accounting.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that an assessee following mercantile accounting must offer interest income to tax on accrual basis, irrespective of delayed receipt. Failure to disclose the full accrued amount in the relevant year justified reassessment and addition. articles.title: GST ITC Cannot Be Denied Solely Due to NGTP Supplier Tag
articles.summary: GST authorities are increasingly denying Input Tax Credit (ITC) to buyers based on allegations of fraud in upstream transactions (NGTP suppliers) without proving the buyer's involvement in fraud. Courts have ruled that genuine buyers should not be burdened with proving unknown upstream transactions, emphasizing that ITC cannot be denied solely due to such allegations. This impacts businesses relying on GST compliance and tax authorities' enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The article discusses how GST authorities are increasingly reversing ITC based on upstream NGTP allegations without proving fraud by the immediate buyer. It highlights judicial views that genuine buyers cannot be burdened with proving unknown upstream transactions. The post GST ITC Cannot Be Denied Solely Due to NGTP Supplier Tag appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SC reiterates IBC can’t be used as tool for coercion, debt recovery in breach of contract cases
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India reiterated that the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a tool for coercion or debt recovery in cases that are primarily contractual disputes. The court upheld a ruling by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT), emphasizing that insolvency proceedings should not be invoked for breaches of contract. This decision reinforces the original intent of the IBC to address genuine insolvency situations rather than commercial disagreements.
articles.raw_summary: SC said insolvency proceedings can't be invoked for debt recovery in disputes that are predominantly contractual in nature. In doing so, it upheld an NCLAT ruling.

articles.title: Supreme Court To Hear Plea on Wages for Temple Priests Today
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is scheduled to hear a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) today that seeks a judicial review of the wages, benefits, and rights of temple priests and other workers in religious institutions. The PIL highlights systemic issues in compensation and employment conditions for these workers. The court's intervention could lead to policy changes affecting labour rights and welfare schemes for temple employees. The case underscores the intersection of religious governance and labour poli

articles.summary: Foreign firms can tap the newly opened energy market only in partnership with Indian companies, according to the SHANTI Act that ended six decades of state monopoly in India's nuclear energy industry.

articles.title: J&K&L High Court Grants Bail To Doctor Accused Of Attempting To Commit Rape On Patient During Medical Exam
articles.summary: The High Court of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh has granted bail to a doctor accused of attempting to rape a woman who had approached him for treatment, reiterating that in non-bailable offences not attracting the statutory embargo under Section 480 of the Bharatiya Nagrik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, bail is the rule and jail is the exception. The Court was hearing a successive bail application filed under Section 483 BNSS by Dr. Abdul Majeed Bhat in connection with FIR for offences under Sections 62 a

articles.title: Kerala AAR Rejects GST Advance Ruling Plea Due to Hypothetical Queries
articles.summary: The Kerala Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) rejected a GST advance ruling plea because it was based on hypothetical scenarios rather than an actual or proposed business transaction. The AAR ruled that such applications must relate to real or intended supplies to be considered valid. This decision impacts how businesses seek tax clarity in Kerala. The ruling underscores the need for advance ruling applications to address concrete business situations.
articles.raw_summary: The Kerala AAR held that advance ruling applications cannot be based on hypothetical scenarios or academic questions. The Authority rejected the application because it did not relate to any actual or proposed supply undertaken by the applicant. The post Kerala AAR Rejects GST Advance Ruling Plea Due to Hypothetical Queries appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SIM-binding will be ‘paired’ with regular logout requirements
articles.summary: The government is extending the implementation of SIM-binding regulations, which require mobile users to link their SIM cards to unique identifiers. Industry stakeholders have raised concerns about the negative impact on user experience, leading to delays in enforcement. The move aims to enhance security and traceability but faces pushback over practical challenges.

articles.title: ET Graphics: BoP stability tested by outflows & Rupee fall
articles.summary: India's external accounts are under strain. The current account deficit is widening, and capital may flow out. The rupee has weakened significantly. Policymakers are considering various measures beyond interest rate hikes to stabilize the currency. These include incentives for foreign deposits and liberalizing capital inflows. State-owned banks may also issue foreign currency bonds.

State-owned banks may also issue foreign currency bonds.

articles.title: 'Bail is rule, jail exception even in UAPA cases': SC expresses reservations over order denying bail to Umar Khalid
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India expressed reservations over an order denying bail to Umar Khalid in a UAPA case, emphasizing that 'bail is the rule, jail is the exception' even in UAPA matters. The case involves Umar Khalid, and the court's stance highlights concerns over stringent bail provisions under UAPA. This ruling has implications for civil liberties and the interpretation of bail laws in India.

articles.title: Mehbooba Mufti Calls for Dialogue, Connectivity and Healing As the Path to J&K’s Future
articles.summary: PDP president Mehbooba Mufti has urged the central government to restore cross-border trade between India and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir and release detained individuals ahead of Eid. She emphasized dialogue, connectivity, and healing as pathways for Jammu & Kashmir’s future. The demands highlight ongoing political and civil liberties concerns in the region.
articles.raw_summary: PDP chief urges Centre to revive cross-LoC trade and release detainees before Eid

articles.title: Global rice prices poised to gain on deficit, geopolitical crisis and weather concerns
articles.summary: Global rice production is projected to drop by 5 million tonnes while consumption could rise by 3.8 million tonnes, driven by geopolitical tensions and adverse weather. This deficit is expected to push rice prices higher worldwide. The situation may have implications for India's food security and agricultural trade policies, given the country's significant role in the global rice market.
articles.raw_summary: Production projected to drop by 5 million tonnes, while consumption could rise by 3.8 million tonnes

articles.title: High-level inquiry committee set up to probe fire in Rajdhani Express: Railway Board
articles.summary: The Railway Board has formed a high-level inquiry committee to investigate a fire incident in the Rajdhani Express. The fire damaged the luggage brake and generator car behind one of the coaches. The Railway Board, a central government body overseeing rail operations, is leading the probe to determine the cause and prevent future occurrences.

During the visit, he will address a public meeting in Khiron and participate in a 'mahila samvaad' (women's dialogue) program in Lalganj. articles.title: Woman killed, 20 persons injured as bus overturns in Chhattisgarh's Bastar
articles.summary: A private bus traveling from Raipur to Sukma overturned in Chhattisgarh's Bastar district in the early hours, killing one woman and injuring 20 others. The accident occurred on a route between the state capital and a Maoist-affected area. Police officials confirmed the incident, but no government policy or regulatory action is mentioned. The cause of the overturning is not specified in the report.
articles.raw_summary: The accident took place in the early hours when the private bus was heading from the State capital Raipur to Sukma, says a police official

articles.summary: Security forces in Manipur have launched operations to rescue people still held captive by armed groups. A senior officer stated that area domination exercises and search operations are underway to rescue all missing persons and arrest those responsible. The actions aim to address ongoing security challenges in the region.

articles.title: In Bihar, a plate of woe
articles.summary: A suspected snake was found in a midday meal served to students in Bihar’s Saharsa district, causing widespread fear among children and parents. Despite assurances from teachers, cooks, and officials that the food is safe, many students have stopped eating lunch at school. The incident highlights concerns about food safety in government-run school meal programs and the psychological impact on vulnerable children.
articles.raw_summary: A week after a suspected snake was found in a midday meal, students in Bihar’s Saharsa district remain too scared to eat lunch at school. Even as teachers, cooks, and education officials insist the food is safe, fear continues to linger among the children and their parents

articles.title: China agrees to boost trade for U.S. agricultural products following Trump-Xi summit
articles.summary: China has agreed to increase imports of U.S. agricultural products following a summit between President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping. The deal aims to ease the impact of the ongoing U.S.-China trade war on American farmers. The announcement was made two days after Trump's visit to Beijing.
articles.raw_summary: The announcement came two days after President Donald Trump returned from a high-stakes summit in Beijing where he sought to ease the impact on American farmers from the trade war he launched last year

articles.title: ‘Engines break down repeatedly’: Two months before Narmada cruise tragedy, MP government got a warning
articles.summary: Two months before a fatal cruise boat accident on the Narmada River, the Madhya Pradesh government received a warning about repeated engine breakdowns in the vessels. The incident raises concerns about safety regulations and oversight of tourism infrastructure. The article suggests the state government was alerted to systemic mechanical failures but does not specify whether corrective action was taken.

articles.summary: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh has written to Union Minister Jual Oram alleging that the Great Nicobar Project violates tribal rights. Ramesh claims the project shows 'flagrant violation' of rights of indigenous communities. The letter demands accountability from the government regarding the project's impact on tribal populations. The Great Nicobar Project involves significant infrastructure development in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

articles.summary: The All India Muslim Personal Law Board (AIMPLB) announced plans to challenge the High Court's Bhojshala verdict in the Supreme Court, arguing that the Archaeological Survey of India's (ASI) past stance contradicts the facts. The dispute centers around the legal status of the Bhojshala site, with implications for religious and civil liberties. The AIMPLB's move reflects broader concerns over the protection of minority rights in India.

articles.title: CJI Surya Kant says time has come for judiciary to work like hospitals 24x7
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant stated that the judiciary should operate on a 24x7 basis, similar to hospitals, to improve access to justice. The statement underscores the need for systemic reforms in the judicial system to ensure timely and efficient legal recourse. The CJI emphasized that this model could address delays and enhance public trust in the judiciary. The remarks were made during a discussion on judicial reforms and efficiency.

articles.title: Election Commission announces third SIR phase in 16 states, three Union Territories
articles.summary: The Election Commission announced the third phase of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) exercise in 16 states and three Union Territories. The exercise in Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, and Ladakh will be delayed due to adverse weather conditions and the ongoing Census. The poll panel stated these reasons for the postponement.
articles.raw_summary: The exercise in Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh will be conducted later because of adverse weather and the Census, the poll panel said.

articles.title: Have serious reservations: Supreme Court critiques own order denying Umar Khalid bail
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed serious reservations about its own previous order that denied bail to Umar Khalid. The court criticized the denial of bail in this case, which involves allegations related to the 2020 Delhi riots. The ruling has implications for legal precedents on bail and detention in similar cases. The exact wording of the court's critique was not provided in the summary.
articles.raw_summary: Have serious reservations: Supreme Court critiques own order denying Umar Khalid bail

articles.title: Karnataka Introduces AIB-based Excise System, Revises Liquor Prices
articles.summary: Karnataka has introduced an AIB-based excise duty system for liquor from May 11, 2026, replacing the previous structure. The new system is globally recognized as a standard for alcohol taxation. Alongside this, the state has revised liquor prices. This change aims to modernize tax collection and align with best practices in alcohol regulation.
articles.raw_summary: The AIB-based excise duty structure has been implemented in Karnataka from May 11, 2026. It is globally recognised as the gold standard for alcohol taxation

The draft has received Cabinet approval and will be introduced during the upcoming legislative session. The UCC aims to unify personal laws for all citizens, though exemptions for certain communities or groups may apply.

articles.title: India's demand-side push could build USD 37.8 billion forex shield as high oil prices threaten fiscal stability: Brickwork Ratings
articles.summary: With crude prices likely to stay above USD 100 per barrel for the rest of 2026 and the rupee under pressure near INR 95/USD, Brickwork Ratings says Prime Minister Modi's seven behavioural appeals could provide India with a USD 37.8 billion forex buffer this fiscal year. The report argues that voluntary demand reduction across fuel, gold and fertilizers may offer the government fiscal breathing room at a time when its ability to absorb oil price pass-through is thinning.

The report argues that voluntary demand reduction across fuel, gold and fertilizers may offer the government fiscal breathing room at a time when its ability to absorb oil price pass-through is thinning.

articles.title: GST Tribunal revamps appeal process: Division bench review now mandatory for appeals
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Tribunal has revised its appeal process, making it mandatory for smaller benches to refer cases involving important legal questions to a division bench. This move aims to ensure consistent rulings and streamline dispute resolution. The decision is part of efforts to strengthen the GST framework and reduce inconsistencies in tax-related legal interpretations.
articles.raw_summary: The move seeks to prevent smaller benches from deciding cases involving important legal questions, ensure consistent rulings, and streamline the process of resolving disputes.

articles.title: IFF’s Representation to Meta against "Automatic Blocking”IFF writes to Meta with regards to news reporting on automatic blocking practices04 May, 20266 min readJhanvi Anam
articles.summary: The Internet Freedom Foundation (IFF) wrote to Meta to raise concerns about its 'automatic blocking' practices affecting news reporting. articles.title: In Himachal Pradesh, tourism and migration are altering beliefs that kept forests safe
articles.summary: Traditional sacred groves in Himachal Pradesh, protected by local beliefs, are facing threats from tourism and migration. These forest patches, preserved for generations, are losing their cultural significance, raising concerns about long-term environmental conservation. The shift could impact biodiversity and indigenous practices tied to forest protection.
articles.raw_summary: Belief-based practices of sacred groves have helped preserve forest patches across generations.

articles.title: RTC employees’ union opposes privatisation move
articles.summary: The employees' union of the Road Transport Corporation (RTC) has opposed the government's move to privatise RTC assets and operations under the guise of electric bus services. The union has called for a round-table meeting with trade unions in Vijayawada on May 26 to decide future actions. The union accuses the government of handing over valuable assets to private companies. This reflects broader concerns about privatisation and its impact on public sector jobs and services.
articles.raw_summary: Accusing the government of trying to hand over valuable assets and operations to private companies in the name of electric bus services, it calls for a round-table with various trade unions in Vijayawada on May 26 to decide the future course of action

articles.title: Disputed Bhojshala site is Saraswati temple, rules MP HC; directs Centre, ASI to manage affairs
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court ruled that the disputed Bhojshala site in Dhar is a Saraswati temple and directed the Centre and Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) to manage its affairs. The court also asked the government to consider allocating separate land in Dhar for a mosque, as sought by the Muslim side. The Hindu side had previously demanded exclusive rights to worship at the site. The ruling has implications for religious rights and governance of disputed sites in India.
articles.raw_summary: Hindu side had sought exclusive rights to worship there. HC also asks govt to consider applications for separate land in Dhar for mosque.

articles.title: Red alert: 15 stocks most vulnerable to FII selling as DIIs refuse to step in
articles.summary: Foreign institutional investors have divested approximately $53 billion from Indian equities since September 2024, leading to significant ownership shifts. Fifteen large-cap stocks, including HDFC Bank and Bharti Airtel, remain highly vulnerable due to dominant FII holdings and a lack of domestic institutional support, despite overall DII market dominance.
articles.raw_summary: Foreign institutional investors have divested approximately $53 billion from Indian equities since September 2024, leading to significant ownership shifts. articles.title: Global Market: China’s April growth slows sharply as consumption, factory output weaken
articles.summary: China's economy slowed sharply in April due to weaker industrial output, retail sales, and investment. Higher energy costs and weak domestic demand were key factors, while exports provided some support. The ongoing property market downturn further impacted growth. This slowdown could influence global trade dynamics, potentially affecting India's economic policies or trade relations.
articles.raw_summary: China's economy shows signs of slowing down. Industrial output, retail sales, and investment activity weakened in April. This slowdown is linked to higher energy costs and subdued domestic demand. Exports offered some support, but consumer spending remains a concern. The property market downturn also continues to impact the economy.

articles.title: Silver tanks Rs 5,600/kg, gold slips Rs 1,000/10g after drone strike on UAE nuclear plant amid Iran war fears. Time to sell?
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices fell sharply after a drone strike on a UAE nuclear plant heightened Iran war fears, pushing oil prices up. This surge in oil prices could keep global interest rates high, affecting India's inflation and monetary policy outlook. Investors are monitoring the U.S. Federal Reserve's next moves for clues on rate decisions.
articles.raw_summary: Gold and silver prices opened sharply lower on Monday, driven by escalating Middle East tensions that pushed oil prices higher and fueled inflation concerns. This surge in oil prices strengthened expectations that interest rates could remain elevated for longer, impacting bullion markets. Investors are closely watching for further clues on the U.S. Federal Reserve's monetary policy outlook.

articles.title: Rupee hits record low of 96.18 vs USD as rising yields add to oil shock
articles.summary: The Indian rupee hit a new all-time low against the dollar on Monday, plummeting to 96.18. Soaring global bond yields, fueled by elevated energy prices from the Iran war, have weakened investor confidence. This marks the rupee's fifth consecutive session of record lows, making it Asia's worst-performing currency this year.

This marks the rupee's fifth consecutive session of record lows, making it Asia's worst-performing currency this year.

articles.title: Pak forces kill 35 terrorists in Balochistan operation, capture 3 high-profile commanders
articles.summary: Pakistan's forces conducted an operation in Balochistan, killing 35 terrorists and capturing three high-profile commanders. The operation was based on intelligence from previously detained militants. The captured commanders are described as significant figures by the Balochistan government spokesperson. This event may impact India's security assessments and diplomatic stance toward Pakistan.

articles.title: One soundbox soon for all payment apps as NPCI readies platform
articles.summary: NPCI is creating a single, interoperable soundbox system for UPI transactions to replace the need for merchants to use multiple payment devices. This initiative aims to reduce costs for small businesses and streamline payment confirmations across all apps. The move is expected to improve efficiency in India's digital payments landscape. NPCI is the organization behind UPI, India's widely used digital payment system.

This move could significantly reduce costs for small businesses and streamline payment confirmations across all apps, fostering greater efficiency in the digital payments ecosystem.

articles.title: Kerala prepares for V D Satheesan swearing-in; CM-designate meets Cardinal Cleemis
articles.summary: Congress leader V D Satheesan is set to be sworn in as Kerala's chief minister on Monday, marking the return of the United Democratic Front (UDF) to power after a decade. Ahead of the ceremony, Satheesan met with Cardinal Baselios Cleemis in Thiruvananthapuram, while cabinet discussions continued. The event signifies a change in state leadership with potential policy shifts for Kerala.

articles.summary: India has introduced new rules for silver imports. Importers now need a government license. These changes affect all silver imports, including those plated with gold and platinum.

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Duplicate Capital Gains Addition Due to Amended Sale Deed Error
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that two sale deeds represented the same transaction because one was an amendment correcting a survey number error. As a result, the ITAT directed the deletion of a duplicate capital gains addition made by the Assessing Officer. The decision clarifies how amended sale deeds should be treated for tax purposes, reducing potential tax disputes for taxpayers.

articles.title: Less than 1% public vehicles have trackers, panic buttons. SC says no fitness certificate without them
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that public vehicles must have location tracking devices and emergency panic buttons installed to receive a fitness certificate. This requirement was mandated under the Motor Vehicles Act in 2018, following the Nirbhaya case. Currently, less than 1% of public vehicles comply with this rule. The court emphasized that no fitness certificate will be issued without these safety measures.
articles.raw_summary: Location tracking devices and emergency buttons had been made mandatory under Motor Vehicles Act in 2018, two years after Nirbhaya case.

articles.summary: Two workers involved in a Noida protest over wage demands have been booked under the National Security Act (NSA) for allegedly inciting violence. The incident highlights concerns over the use of stringent laws in labour disputes and raises questions about police action and protest rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: The family of one of the persons stated that she had been picked up by the police two days before the agitation for increased wages turned violent.

articles.title: Ordinance to up SC judges from 33 to 37 promulgated
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu promulgated an ordinance to increase the number of Supreme Court judges from 33 to 37, raising the total to 38 including the Chief Justice of India. This move aims to address the backlog of over 90,000 pending cases in the apex court. The ordinance will be replaced by a bill during the upcoming monsoon session of Parliament.

articles.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a global health emergency
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared a global health emergency due to an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. The outbreak raises concerns for neighboring countries, including India, due to potential cross-border spread and the need for coordinated international health responses.

articles.title: NSE's Electronic Gold Receipts: What are EGRs and what is in them for gold buyers?
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) has introduced Electronic Gold Receipts (EGRs), allowing investors to hold gold in electronic form instead of physical form. This enables buyers to purchase small quantities (as little as 100 milligrams) and benefit from gold price movements. The system ensures gold purity and allows conversion back to physical gold or sale. The initiative is part of India's broader push toward digital financial instruments under SEBI's regulatory framework.
articles.raw_summary: EGRs give people the option to convert their physical gold into electronic format, hold it and enjoy the upside in gold prices, and later sell it or convert back to physical gold. The gold’s purity is assured and can be bought in small quantities of 100 milligrams.

articles.title: Lowdown: What is the landmark US cryptocurrency regulation passed by the Senate Banking Committee
articles.summary: The US Senate Banking Committee advanced the CLARITY Act of 2025 in a bipartisan 15-9 vote, establishing regulatory rules for digital assets. The bill splits oversight between the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). This marks a significant step in US cryptocurrency regulation, which could set a precedent for other countries, including India, in shaping their own digital asset policies.

articles.title: Kharif output dips by over 10% across states in El Niño years: Study
articles.summary: El Niño's shadow looms over India's monsoon. Past El Niño years saw key kharif crops like paddy and maize suffer over 10% output reduction in numerous districts. This study highlights vulnerable areas and stresses the need for district-level climate-resilient planning. Policymakers are urged to strengthen contingency plans for drought-tolerant varieties and efficient water management to protect farmers.

articles.title: CBSE slashes fees for Class 12 re-evaluation
articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has reduced fees for re-evaluating Class 12 exam papers amid concerns from students about result accuracy. The board has also promised full refunds if marks increase after re-evaluation, aiming to ensure transparency and support for students. This decision addresses student anxiety over potential errors in evaluation.

articles.title: 'No wrongdoing... justice will prevail': Union minister breaks silence after son arrested in POCSO case
articles.summary: A Union Minister whose son has been arrested in a POCSO (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences) case has broken his silence, stating that justice will prevail. The developments have deeply affected his family. The case involves child rights, a civil liberties issue, and may lead to broader discussions on legal enforcement and governance.
articles.raw_summary: In a detailed post on X, Kumar described how the developments surrounding the case had deeply affected his family members.

articles.title: Shutdown over church leaders’ killings in Manipur strands 300 vehicles on NH-2
articles.summary: A shutdown in Manipur’s Senapati and Kangpokpi districts, triggered by the killings of church leaders, has stranded 300 vehicles on National Highway 2. The disruption has halted vehicular movement in the region, raising concerns over security and governance. The incident involves tribal and religious tensions, with potential implications for state intervention or policy measures to address violence and restore order.

articles.title: Tax wealthy, not fuel and gold: Former UN Advisor, economist Santosh Mehrotra warns of Rupee slump, job losses
articles.summary: Former UN Advisor Santosh Mehrotra urged the government to tax the super-rich instead of relying on indirect taxes on fuel and gold, warning these are fueling inflation and job losses. He cautioned that escalating geopolitical tensions could push the Indian Rupee to ₹100 against the US dollar within a quarter, exacerbating economic stress.

articles.title: Arm Holdings to face US antitrust probe over chip tech: Report
articles.summary: The US Federal Trade Commission is investigating Arm Holdings over allegations that the company may be illegally monopolizing parts of the semiconductor market through its chip technology licensing practices. The probe is part of broader global scrutiny of Arm's business practices, with similar investigations underway in other countries. Arm is also engaged in a dispute with Qualcomm. The outcome of this probe could impact the semiconductor industry and trade policies worldwide.

Regulators want to know if Arm is illegally monopolizing parts of the semiconductor market. This scrutiny is part of global attention on Arm's business practices. Arm faces similar investigations in other countries. A dispute with Qualcomm is also ongoing.

articles.title: Maharashtra cuts VAT on aviation fuel to 7% for six months amid fuel cost surge
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government reduced the Value Added Tax (VAT) on aviation turbine fuel (ATF) from 18% to 7% for six months. This decision aims to ease financial pressure on airlines amid rising fuel costs and disruptions linked to the West Asia crisis. The move is expected to lower operational costs for airlines operating in the state.

So far, 16 individuals have been arrested. The case raises concerns about regulatory oversight and financial governance in India's banking sector.

articles.title: Southwest monsoon reaches Andaman and Nicobar region ahead of Kerala onset: IMD
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported that the southwest monsoon has reached the Andaman and Nicobar Islands ahead of its expected onset in Kerala on May 26. The monsoon's advance over the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and the Andaman region is seen as favorable for further progress in the coming days. This development is significant for India's agricultural planning and water resource management, as the monsoon plays a crucial role in the country's farming cycles and overall economy.
articles.raw_summary: The southwest monsoon has advanced into parts of the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal and the Andaman region, the IMD said, adding that conditions remain favourable for further progress over the next few days ahead of the expected Kerala onset on May 26.

articles.title: Elderly Andhra couple protesting land takeover lifted using excavator
articles.summary: An elderly couple in Andhra Pradesh was forcibly removed from their land using an excavator while protesting its acquisition for an airport project. The incident has sparked local outrage and raised concerns about the rights of vulnerable groups and the methods used in land acquisition. The protest underscores broader issues of governance and civil liberties in infrastructure development.
articles.raw_summary: An elderly couple protesting land takeover for an airport in Andhra Pradesh was forcibly moved by an excavator, sparking outrage among locals.

articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a significant increase. Reserves rose by over six billion US dollars in the week ending May 8. This surge brought the total reserves to nearly 700 billion US dollars. The rise was driven by increases in foreign currency assets and gold reserves. This marks a positive development for the country's financial standing.

articles.title: Odisha says fuel shortage caused by panic buying, assures normal supply in 48 hours

articles.summary: India's unemployment rate climbed to 5.2% in April, a six-month peak. Rural joblessness saw an increase, while urban unemployment eased to a year-low. Urban female unemployment declined significantly. Youth unemployment edged up, with a sharp rise for young women. Labour force participation and the worker population ratio also saw a slight decrease.

articles.title: India spots an export champion in a sector tariffs failed to sink

The government is now considering a production-linked incentive scheme to further boost value-added exports and expand the exporter base, solidifying India's position as a global powerhouse.

articles.summary: India’s retail inflation rose to a 13-month high of 3.48% in April, driven by higher food and services prices, though the headline print remained below the 4% mark. Food inflation accelerated to 4.20% from 3.87% in March, while core inflation edged up to 3.4%.
articles.raw_summary: India’s retail inflation rose to 3.48% in April from 3.40% in March, driven by higher food and services prices, though the reading remained below CNBC-TV18’s poll estimate of 3.72%. The latest print marks a 13-month high under the revised CPI series, while under the older series it would have been a four-month high.

articles.title: Oil prices set for weekly gain with Iran war resolution at deadlock

The deadlock in resolving the Iran conflict could impact global oil supply and prices, which in turn affects India's energy costs and economic stability. The situation is being closely monitored for potential policy responses by the Indian government regarding fuel pricing or strategic reserves.

articles.title: Gold, Silver imports to cost more after customs duty, cess hike: Details here
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the customs duty and cess on gold and silver imports from 6% to 10%, and imposed a 10% duty on platinum and related precious metal products. articles.raw_summary: With this increase, the total duty on gold and silver now stands at 10% from 6% earlier. Platinum, and related precious metal products, including hooks, clasps, clamps, pins and screw backs used in manufacturing, will also have a 10% duty.

articles.summary: The Indian government has approved the installation of 1.3 million rooftop solar systems under the ULA (User-Led Aggregation) model across 10 states and Union Territories. This initiative aims to expand solar energy adoption in residential and small-scale sectors. This move allows Prudential to deploy a wider product suite and leverage Bharti's domestic reach, aiming to boost insurance penetration in India.

articles.title: Weather & then war lead to tears in India’s onion basket
articles.summary: Maharashtra's onion farmers face devastating losses as prices plummet far below production costs. A combination of bumper harvests, weak demand, export disruptions, and unseasonal rains has led to crops rotting and farmers selling at a significant deficit. Many are demanding government intervention and compensation for their distress.

articles.title: India among fastest-growing steel market as global prices rise: Goldman Sachs
articles.summary: Global steel prices surged in April and early May, with India emerging as a fast-growing market. Hot rolled coil prices rose across major regions, led by Brazil. India's crude steel production saw an 11% year-on-year increase in March, outpacing global trends. China's steel output contracted, though capacity cuts are expected to be delayed.

articles.title: India jewellery exports fall 9.07 per cent in April to Rs 20.82 crore amid geopolitical tensions
articles.summary: India's jewellery exports saw a significant drop in April. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia and market uncertainty impacted sales. Exports of cut and polished diamonds, lab-grown diamonds, and gold jewellery declined. However, silver jewellery exports experienced a substantial surge. This trend reflects global economic and political influences on the sector.

articles.title: FPI selling: Foreign investors pull Rs 27,000 crore in May; 2026 outflows cross Rs 2.2 lakh crore mark
articles.summary: Foreign investors have continued their withdrawal from Indian equities, with net outflows reaching Rs 27,048 crore this month. This selling spree, totaling Rs 2.2 lakh crore in 2026, is driven by global macroeconomic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions, and a stronger US dollar making developed markets more attractive.

Two others were critically injured. The incident raises concerns about safety measures for workers under government employment programs.

articles.title: TMC's Mahua Moitra and Kirti Azad join ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ amid row over CJI remarks
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress (TMC) leaders Mahua Moitra and Kirti Azad have joined an unofficial, satirical political group called the 'Cockroach Janta Party' amid controversy over remarks made by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant. The group, which has 40,000 members, emerged as a form of political satire in response to the CJI's comments. The development highlights public reaction to judicial statements and may contribute to broader discussions on governance and accountability in India.

articles.title: UP emerges as key target in Pak-linked digital radicalisation, sleeper-cell network probe
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh has been identified as a key target for Pakistan-linked digital radicalisation and sleeper-cell networks due to its large youth population, widespread smartphone access, and rapid spread of social media. The state's vulnerabilities raise concerns about national security and may prompt government or regulatory action to address cyber threats and radicalisation. The issue highlights the intersection of technology, governance, and civil liberties in India's policy landscape.
articles.raw_summary: UP has become increasingly vulnerable because of its large youth population, widespread smartphone access and the rapid spread of social media networks.

articles.title: Dating app match, barely six-month marriage: How ‘dowry death’ victim met her Bhopal husband
articles.summary: A woman who met her husband through a dating app married in early 2024 and moved to Bhopal to live with his family. She later died under suspicious circumstances, with her family alleging dowry harassment. The case has drawn attention to the persistence of dowry-related violence in India, despite legal prohibitions. Authorities are investigating the circumstances of her death.
articles.raw_summary: After the wedding, the woman shifted to Bhopal to live with her husband and his family at their residence in the Bagh Mugalia Extension-Katara Hills area.

articles.title: First Saraswati idol installed at Bhojshala sanctum; posters outside ban entry of non-Hindus
articles.summary: The Bhojshala Utsav Samiti and Bhojshala Mukti Yagna Samiti installed a Saraswati idol at the Bhojshala sanctum and put up posters outside the temple barring non-Hindus from entering. This action has raised concerns about religious freedom and minority rights, as the site is historically significant and subject to legal disputes. The groups involved are asserting Hindu religious claims over the site, which could escalate tensions or prompt legal or administrative responses.
